_comment: Cached Tavily-style grounded facts for the Euphronios Krater, used when DEMO_MODE=mock or live search fails. Every fact carries a source URL.

results.title: Euphronios Krater — The Met (formerly), repatriated to Italy 2008
results.content: The Metropolitan Museum of Art acquired the Euphronios (Sarpedon) krater in 1972 for $1 million. Following Italy's evidence of illicit excavation, the Met agreed in 2006 to transfer title; the krater was returned to Italy in January 2008.

results.title: Sarpedon Krater displayed at Villa Giulia / Cerveteri
results.content: Repatriated antiquities including the Euphronios krater are associated with Etruscan sites near Cerveteri; UNESCO frameworks on illicit trafficking of cultural property (1970 Convention) underpin such returns.

results.title: Acquisition controversy and the Medici conviction
results.content: Press and registry coverage tied the krater's surfacing in the early 1970s to dealer networks later subject to looting investigations; provenance before 1971 is undocumented.
